description: High-performance System.Text.Json reference serialization for .NET: serialize object graphs with shared objects into JSON where each duplicate becomes an RFC 6901 JSON Pointer $ref to the object's real location, and deserialize such documents back with reference identity preserved. Built for very large documents: ~5.9x faster and ~5.7x less allocation than a JsonNode-walking baseline on a 6.6 MB production document.
rules: Create one PooledReferenceSerializer<T>/PooledReferenceDeserializer<T> per (JsonSerializerOptions, type) pair and reuse it (static field); leases handle per-operation state.
Input to the transformer must be ReferenceHandler.Preserve-shaped: a $ref never precedes its $id; forward references pass through untransformed.
Reference ids must not require JSON escaping; the encoder must not escape / or ~ in property names (default and UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping are safe).
PooledReferenceHandler/PoolingReferenceResolver are single-operation: reset between documents or use a fresh instance.
Prefer the PipeWriter overload on hot paths; use JsonTypeInfo<T> overloads under trimming or Native AOT.
